de.uka.ilkd.key.smt.newsmt2

Class DefinedSymbolsHandler

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.smt.newsmt2.DefinedSymbolsHandler
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    SMTHandler

    public class DefinedSymbolsHandler
extends java.lang.Object
implements SMTHandler
    This handler is meant to translate all function symbols for which key axioms, SMT axioms or defining taclets have been registered.

    Adding new function symbols

    New functions can be registered in a file XXX.DefinedSymbolsHandler.preamble.xml for some prefix XXX. This file must be located in the resources for the package in which this class resides.

    Such an xml file is not an actual xml file but rather an xml fragment consisting of a set of entries to be used as axiomatisation when the SMT translation is triggered. Three kind of entries are possible for a function symbol f:

    1. f.axioms: Specify SMTLib code that will be added as an assertion to the resulting SMT code. This cannot be checked for correctness, however
    2. f.taclets: Specify a (comma-separated) list of builtin KeY taclets whose meaning formula will be translated as axiomatisation. Since the taclets are available in KeY, the translation is as correct as the KeY calculus in this point.
    3. f.dl: Specify a DL formula to specify an axiom. The formula will be translated to SMTLib and used as axiomatisation. There is a test case in the testing framework that requires that all such axiomatisation is proved within KeY. If more than one DL axiom is required, the next ones are called f.dl.2, f.dl.3, etc.

    Triggers in DL formulae

    When specifying DL formulae, you can use the <<Trigger>> term label to specify the subterm which should be used as a trigger (:pattern) in SMTLib to help Z3 (and other solvers) to instantiate the quantified variables suitably. 
         \forall Seq s; seqLen(s)<<Trigger>> >= 0
    is the axiom for seqLen (hence stored in seqLen.dl). The trigger pattern to be used for instantiation is seqLen(s) matching against any ground instance of seqLen.

    • Method Detail

      • init

        public void init(MasterHandler masterHandler,
                 Services services,
                 java.util.Properties handlerSnippets)
          throws java.io.IOException
        Description copied from interface: SMTHandler
        Initialise this handler. This method is only called once after creation and before any other method is called. This method may also allocate additional resources that it needs for translation.
        Specified by:
        init in interface SMTHandler
        services - the non-null services object which is relevant for this handler
        handlerSnippets - the snippets loaded for this handler, null if no snippet property file is available for this handler
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if resources cannot be read.

      • canHandle

        public boolean canHandle(Operator op)
        Description copied from interface: SMTHandler
        Query if this handler can translate an operator. Test if this handler can translate any term with the given argument top level operator.
        Specified by:
        canHandle in interface SMTHandler
        Parameters:
        op - a non-null operator to translate
        Returns:
        true if this handler can successfully translate all terms with op as toplevel operator

      • handle

        public SExpr handle(MasterHandler trans,
                    Term term)
             throws SMTTranslationException
        Description copied from interface: SMTHandler
        Translate the given term into an SMT SExpression. This method will only be called if SMTHandler.canHandle(Term) returned true for the same term argument. The translation may add to the set of assumptions and declarations using corresponding calls to the MasterHandler that it receives.
        Specified by:
        handle in interface SMTHandler
        Parameters:
        trans - the non-null master handler to which it belongs
        term - the non-null term to translate
        Returns:
        an SExpr representing the term
        Throws:
        SMTTranslationException - if the translation fails unexpectedly.

      • getProperties

        public java.util.List<SMTHandlerProperty<?>> getProperties()
        Description copied from interface: SMTHandler
        Any handler can offer a collection of properties that can be set in the GUI/script to control the translation. The returned list is used in SMTHandlerServices and will not be modified.
        Specified by:
        getProperties in interface SMTHandler
        Returns:
        the non-null list of properties that this handler supports and reads.
